title: fraxinus excelsior subsp. angustifolia
intro: Fraxinus excelsior subsp. angustifolia, also known as the narrow-leaved ash, is a subspecies of the ash tree that is native to Europe. The bark, leaves, and seeds of the narrow-leaved ash have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving joint pain, and supporting digestive health, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Fraxinus excelsior subsp. angustifolia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
